risk_responses.risk: Interface change breaks formatOutput, parseOutput, NextAffordanceSchema
risk_responses.resolution: EXTEND NextAffordance (add when, args as OPTIONAL fields). Do NOT rename or remove action, target, command. The interface becomes additive-only. formatOutput (L371-373) continues to read action, target, command — unchanged. New when and args are serialized in JSON.stringify (L375) automatically and round-tripped by parseOutput.

risk_responses.risk: suggestedNextTools and nextAffordances collision in formatOutput
risk_responses.resolution: Keep both streams as complementary layers: suggestedNextTools = category-based simple list, nextAffordances = tool-specific structured guidance. The LLM receives both; they target different reasoning modes (quick suggestions vs detailed chains). No consolidation needed — additive information is not collision, it's enrichment.

risk_responses.risk: Ad-hoc next/hint fields in 7 tools collide with standardized next
risk_responses.resolution: Phase 3 explicitly migrates each ad-hoc field. For roadmap: rename 'next' items array to 'items' to free the 'next' key. For smart_delegate: replace ad-hoc 'next' string and 'hint' strings with standardized next array. For fragment, task_board, smart_session, replace_ symbol, smart_edit, smart_stats, smart_batch: replace ad-hoc hint/next/recommendation strings with standard next: NextAffordance[].
Fix-B adds exact-match lookup table as first dispatch in toolCategory(), before substring fallbacks. All 30 tools get explicit categories. The diagnostic fallback remains for future unknown tool IDs.
Not adding to TOOL_CATEGORIES constant. Per-tool affordances live in affordancesAfterSuccess() as a local Record, not in the shared constant. TOOL_CATEGORIES remains category-level only.
Cap per-tool nextAffordances at 3 entries max. Use short command strings (<80 chars). Per-tool affordances in affordancesAfterSuccess replace category defaults for recognized tools — no doubling.

risk_responses.risk: parseOutput silent failure on schema mismatch
risk_responses.resolution: Since we're extending (not renaming) fields, old-format JSON still parses: NextAffordance objects with only {action, target?, command?} parse fine because when/args are optional. parseOutput's JSON.parse produces an object where undefined optional fields are absent — safe. No migration needed.

Per-tool affordance specs avoid self-recommending loops. No tool recommends itself except explicit 'retry'. Tool chain graphs are acyclic (smart_find → read_source → smart_grep → read_source — this is a standard search loop, not a pathological cycle).

All affordance action/target values use tool IDs that exist in the 30-tool set: read_source, smart_grep, smart_find, smart_edit, smart_bun, smart_batch, smart_delegate, task_board, fragment, smart_session, roadmap, lsp. No phantom references. If a tool is disabled by permissions, the LLM naturally filters unavailable tools.

No shared affordances.ts module created. Per-tool affordances are inline in each tool's execute() function. Centralized affordances live in typed-result.ts which is imported by session/tools.ts — no new import edges.

Tools that have no natural next actions (verify, feedback, record, analytics) explicitly emit next: []. Tools not yet migrated would have no next field at all. Consumers can distinguish: absent = not yet implemented (use system defaults), empty [] = explicitly no suggestions (trust it).
